metadata.seed: Oh, there's a couple things. My classwork has been really been bogging me down lately, and then while I'm still doing better at work, I'm still not where I want to be. I don't want to have to consciously think this is why I'm. I want to keep working on. And I'm not being so argumentative at work. And then my boyfriend has been, like I said earlier, really gritting on my nerves, and I don't, like, I told him the other day that I wanted to break up with him, but he told me he was going to change and that things would get better. So I'm kind of torn with whether or not to continue in their relationship or just let it go. And at the same time, I'm having a hard time keeping up with my schoolwork and getting everything done on time. And my homework isn't where I want it to be. Like, I feel like I can do better.

patient.query: Oh, there's a couple things. My classwork has been really been bogging me down lately, and then while I'm still doing better at work, I'm still not where I want to be. I don't want to have to consciously think this is why I'm. I want to keep working on. And I'm not being so argumentative at work. And then my boyfriend has been, like I said earlier, really gritting on my nerves, and I don't, like, I told him the other day that I wanted to break up with him, but he told me he was going to change and that things would get better. So I'm kind of torn with whether or not to continue in their relationship or just let it go. And at the same time, I'm having a hard time keeping up with my schoolwork and getting everything done on time. And my homework isn't where I want it to be. Like, I feel like I can do better.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not where I want to be
I don't want to consciously think about this
I might want to break up
Things will get better
I can do better

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re juggling a lot—classwork piling up, feeling stuck at work, and a relationship that’s draining you, all while wondering if you can improve. What part of this feels most urgent to you right now?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re juggling a lot—classwork piling up, feeling stuck at work, and a relationship that’s draining you, all while wondering if you can improve. What part of this feels most urgent to you right now?

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you’re weighing whether staying in the relationship will help you feel better. What evidence do you have that staying will actually lead to the improvement you’re hoping for?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you’re juggling a lot right now. If a close friend were watching you, what might they say about how you’re handling all of that?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you’re feeling overwhelmed by school, work, and your relationship, and that’s understandable. What feels most pressing to you right now?

patient.query: I guess... the classwork. It’s like I can't keep up, and every time I sit down to study, I just feel overwhelmed. It makes me question if I even belong in this program.

automatic_thoughts: I can't keep up
I don't belong in this program

llm_response.response: It sounds like you feel overwhelmed and doubt your place in the program whenever you sit down to study. What thoughts come up when you feel you can’t keep up?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you feel overwhelmed and doubt your place in the program whenever you sit down to study. What thoughts come up when you feel you can’t keep up?

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you feel overwhelmed. What evidence do you have that you can't keep up with the classwork?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Automatic thoughts: I can't keep up, I don't belong
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you’re feeling overwhelmed and doubting your place in the program. If a close friend were watching you tackle this classwork, what might they say about your effort and progress?

safety_check.response: It sounds like the workload feels overwhelming and you’re doubting your fit in the program. What specific thoughts or experiences make you feel you don’t belong?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I don't belong in this program
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

patient.query: It's like this voice in my head that says I'm not smart enough or that everyone else is doing better than me. I just… I feel really anxious, and then I just freeze. It’s hard to focus or even start.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not smart enough
everyone else is doing better than me

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like that inner voice is making you feel less capable and anxious, and that can freeze you and make it hard to focus or start. When that voice comes up, what does it feel like for you?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like that inner voice is making you feel less capable and anxious, and that can freeze you and make it hard to focus or start. When that voice comes up, what does it feel like for you?

patient.query: It feels heavy, like this weight on my chest. I get knots in my stomach, and my heart races. It’s almost paralyzing, and I just want to escape from it all. It’s really exhausting just thinking about it.

automatic_thoughts: I want to escape from it all
It's really exhausting just thinking about it

neutral: Anxiety about having a heart attack (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy, tight sensation in your chest and stomach, and that exhaustion is making it feel almost impossible to keep going. What does that feeling of wanting to escape look like for you right now?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy, tight sensation in your chest and stomach, and that exhaustion is making it feel almost impossible to keep going. What does that feeling of wanting to escape look like for you right now?

retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

patient.query: I think it’s just this urge to avoid everything—like skipping classes or putting off assignments. I just want to hide, you know? But then, I end up feeling worse because I know deep down that I need to keep going. It’s like I’m in this cycle that I can’t break.

automatic_thoughts: urge to avoid everything
want to hide
need to keep going

neutral: Fear of not coping with treatment (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re caught in a cycle where avoiding classes and assignments feels like a quick escape, but then you’re left feeling worse because you know you need to keep going. What’s one small thing you think could help you feel a bit more in control right now?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re caught in a cycle where avoiding classes and assignments feels like a quick escape, but then you’re left feeling worse because you know you need to keep going. What’s one small thing you think could help you feel a bit more in control right now?

socratic_questioning.response: What evidence do you have that hiding helps you feel better? If you tried a small step toward a class, what might happen?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you’re stuck in a cycle of avoidance that’s making you feel worse. If a close friend were watching, what might they say about the situation?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you’re caught in a cycle of avoidance that’s leaving you feeling worse, and that can feel exhausting. What might happen if you tried to face one small part of that cycle, like attending one class or starting one assignment?

patient.query: I don’t know... maybe just setting a really small goal, like reading just one page or something? But then I worry that I won’t even be able to do that. It just feels so daunting, and I’m scared of failing again.

automatic_thoughts: I won’t be able to do that
it feels so daunting
I’m scared of failing again

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re considering setting a very small goal, like reading one page, but you’re worried you won’t be able to do it and feel scared of failing again. What would it feel like if you did manage to read that one page?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re considering setting a very small goal, like reading one page, but you’re worried you won’t be able to do it and feel scared of failing again. What would it feel like if you did manage to read that one page?

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you’re worried about failing again. What evidence do you have that you won’t be able to read just one page?

alternative_perspective.response: It might help to imagine what a supportive friend would say about setting a tiny goal like one page. What might that friend suggest to make it feel less daunting?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're feeling anxious about setting a small goal and worried about failing again. What might be a realistic way to start with just one page that feels doable for you?

patient.query: If I actually read that one page... maybe I'd feel a tiny bit accomplished? But then there’s this nagging part of me that thinks, “What if I still can’t understand it?” I guess I’d feel a mix of relief and fear at the same time. It's just hard to shake off that doubt.

automatic_thoughts: What if I still can’t understand it?
maybe I'd feel a tiny bit accomplished

neutral: Difficulty understanding more than three word instructions (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re hoping to feel a little accomplished after reading that page, but you’re also worried you might still not understand it, which is bringing a mix of relief and fear. What might help you feel more confident about getting it?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re hoping to feel a little accomplished after reading that page, but you’re also worried you might still not understand it, which is bringing a mix of relief and fear. What might help you feel more confident about getting it?

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you’re worried about not understanding, but what evidence do you have that you won’t understand the page if you read it?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you’re weighing the possibility of feeling accomplished against the fear of not understanding. If a close friend were watching you, what might they say about how you’re handling this?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you’re feeling a mix of hope and uncertainty about reading that page. What might help you feel more confident about understanding it?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: automatic thoughts: What if I still can’t understand it?
retrieved_concepts_used.label: automatic_thoughts

patient.query: I don't know... maybe if I had someone to explain it to me or help me through it? But then I feel embarrassed asking for help. It’s like I shouldn't need it, you know? That makes me hesitant to reach out, even though I really want to.

automatic_thoughts: I shouldn't need help
I feel hesitant to reach out

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Able to recognize own emotional pain and distress (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re torn between wanting help and feeling embarrassed that you might need it, which makes you hesitant to reach out. What would it feel like if you could share that need with someone you trust?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re torn between wanting help and feeling embarrassed that you might need it, which makes you hesitant to reach out. What would it feel like if you could share that need with someone you trust?

automatic_thoughts: they’d think less of me
I’m not capable
letting that guard down is hard

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Fear of losing control of bowels in public (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re torn between the relief of sharing and the fear that others might see you as less capable, which is understandable. What might be a small step you could consider taking toward sharing?

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you’re worried that opening up might make others view you as less capable. What evidence do you have that people would think less of you if you let your guard down?

patient.query: I guess... maybe they’d say it takes a lot of courage to open up about what I'm feeling. But I still can’t help but think they’d see it as a weakness. It’s like I should have it all together, you know? I want to be strong, not a burden.

automatic_thoughts: they’d see it as a weakness
I should have it all together
I want to be strong, not a burden

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
contradiction: Able to recognize own emotional pain and distress (finding)

llm_response.response:
It sounds like you’re carrying a lot of pressure on yourself—feeling that opening up might be seen as a weakness and that you need to have everything together so you don’t become a burden. That’s a heavy load to bear, and it’s understandable that it would make you feel anxious and ashamed. Many people share that fear of being judged or of losing control when they share their feelings.

What do you think people would see as a weakness if you opened up? And when you imagine that, what evidence do you have that it will actually happen? Sometimes exploring those thoughts can help us see whether they’re based on facts or on assumptions we’ve made about how others will react.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a lot of pressure to appear strong and worried that sharing your feelings might be seen as a weakness. What would it feel like if someone did see your openness as a sign of courage instead?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of being a burden to others
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

socratic_questioning.response: What evidence do you have that sharing your feelings would make you a burden, and what evidence might suggest the opposite?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of being a burden to others
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you’re worried they’ll see vulnerability as weakness, but a close friend might actually admire your courage—what would they say? And maybe you’re already showing strength by opening up.

safety_check.response: It sounds like you’re worried that opening up might be seen as a weakness and that you need to appear strong. What evidence do you have that people would see it that way?
